workflows.name: Brand Foundation Setup
workflows.description: A current brand profile and durable voice guide that future agents can retrieve, apply, test, and update without guessing.
workflows.audience: Founders, builders, and teams defining a recognizable editorial voice
workflows.problem: A generic tone prompt cannot preserve a real point of view, especially after several agents, drafts, and channels touch the work.
workflows.outcome: A current brand profile and durable voice guide that future agents can retrieve, apply, test, and update without guessing.
workflows.whyAi: AI can compare interviews, real writing samples, project experience, and reader reactions at scale, while the operator's expressed intent and supplied evidence remain the authority on identity.
operatorPath: Share the brand goal, audience, representative writing, and any current voice guidance.
Answer a focused interview when the samples do not explain the judgment behind the writing.
Review a focused choice only when the evidence leaves a material identity question unresolved.
Receive a retrieval-tested profile and guide as the content-production baseline.
agentPath: Inventory existing brand resources, guides, writing samples, and content outcomes before proposing changes.
Interview for point of view, lived proof, preferred tension, language habits, boundaries, and what the operator refuses to sound like.
Separate durable voice principles from channel mechanics and brittle phrase blacklists.
Draft the profile and guide, run independent voice review, then finalize and persist the supported foundation.
Re-query the stored profile and artifact to prove future agents can retrieve them.
recovery: If samples conflict, preserve the variants and ask which context each one represents.
If a material identity choice cannot be resolved from the request or evidence, preserve the reviewed draft and ask one focused question.
If persistence or retrieval fails, stop before declaring the foundation ready.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a reviewed draft only when missing authority or context would materially change the identity.
Complete after returning the retrieval-tested content-production handoff; do not start or mutate the next workflow unless the operator separately asks for content production.

handoffs.when: The active brand profile and voice guide have been finalized and retrieval-tested.

prerequisites: A brand or personal-authority goal and target audience
Representative writing samples, prior content, or willingness to be interviewed
Disclosure and sensitive-information boundaries
proof: Voice principles, structure rules, and evidence standard
Anti-generic kill tests applied to examples
Independent voice review and durable finalization decision
whenToUse: A project needs its first durable brand voice and editorial foundation before producing content.
Existing content sounds generic, inconsistent, promotional, or unlike the operator.
The project has voice guidance in another project or document that should be adapted without copying it blindly.

stages.title: Inventory Existing Foundation
stages.summary: Find current profiles, guides, representative content, outcomes, and conflicting guidance before drafting anything new.

stages.title: Interview And Sample Analysis
stages.summary: Capture the operator's point of view and analyze real writing choices only where the current evidence needs it.

stages.title: Draft Brand Foundation
stages.summary: Turn the evidence into a compact profile and practical voice guide that agents can use without imitating a caricature.

stages.title: Adversarial Voice Review
stages.summary: Independently test distinctiveness, fidelity, usability, evidence, and anti-generic quality before activation.

stages.title: Finalize And Persist
stages.summary: Resolve review findings, record the finalization decision, upsert the active brand profile, and create or update the durable voice-guide artifact.

stages.title: Verify Retrieval
stages.summary: Re-query the saved profile and artifact and prove a future content run receives the correct active foundation.

workflows.name: Branding Content Production
workflows.description: A reviewed, evidence-backed piece in the project voice, with channel versions and publication governed by the request's explicit execution intent.
workflows.audience: Builders, founders, and content teams sharing real operating experience
workflows.problem: Useful operating experience often gets flattened into generic content or a disguised product pitch.
workflows.outcome: A reviewed, evidence-backed piece in the project voice, with channel versions and publication governed by the request's explicit execution intent.
workflows.whyAi: AI can connect interviews, project evidence, prior positions, drafting, independent review, and bounded execution while keeping the operator's judgment visible.
operatorPath: Name the topic, audience, useful lesson, and how prominently the product should appear.
Share source boundaries or answer a short interview when the missing experience matters.
Set packet-only, stage, or publish intent and name the target channels when execution is wanted.
Answer a focused question only when missing context would materially change the piece or destination.
agentPath: Load the brand foundation, prior positions, cleared sources, and channel rules.
Decide whether the optional interview is required, useful, or unnecessary; record why when it is skipped.
Research and draft from traceable evidence, then run claim, voice, and sanitization review.
Finalize the packet, then stop, stage, or publish according to publication_intent without adding another human checkpoint.
recovery: If the voice or source boundary is missing, pause for brand-foundation setup.
If evidence conflicts, expose the conflict and ask one bounded decision question.
If publication fails, preserve the final packet and record the channel-specific blocker.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after a review-ready content packet when publication_intent is packet_only.
After the intent-specific terminal condition is met, return closeout without creating unrelated follow-up work or changing another workflow.
prerequisites: An active brand voice profile or representative writing samples
A topic, audience, useful lesson, and product-mention boundary
Approved source and disclosure boundaries
Channel targets only when publication is in scope
proof: Independent claim, voice, and sanitization verdicts
Explicit packet-only, stage, or publish intent
Publication receipts only for selected channels
whenToUse: A solo operator wants one workflow to interview them, research the facts, pull supporting artifacts, shape the idea, draft, review, generate optional images, and publish or stage selected channels.
The user says they want to generate content on a topic, with evidence or source material, using the branding workflow.
The output should include a canonical draft plus X, LinkedIn, website, or email publication without requiring separate workflow runs.
Website or channel imagery may be needed, but generation should happen only after an explicit image plan.

stages.title: Interview Capture
stages.summary: Decide whether first-hand operator context is needed, then ask only the useful journalist-style questions or record why the interview can be skipped.

stages.title: Research And Fact Collection
stages.summary: Use the operator intent, interview decision, any interview pack, and cleared sources to collect and organize facts before angle selection.

stages.title: Angle And Structure
stages.summary: Propose one to three piece ideas, select the structure, name target channels, and decide whether images are needed.

stages.title: Draft Canonical
stages.summary: Write the canonical piece in the adapted operator voice from the selected angle and evidence pack.

stages.title: Editorial Review
stages.summary: Run one compact review pass for factual support, prior-position consistency, voice, and channel form.

stages.title: Sanitization Review
stages.summary: Apply disclosure policy separately and fail closed on public-use risk.

stages.title: Produce Optional Images
stages.summary: Resolve the selected image set, generating assets only when the image plan requires provider-generated website or channel media.

stages.title: Render Channel Publication Jobs
stages.summary: Produce executable website, X, LinkedIn, and optional email publication payloads plus one channel-ready publication bundle from the canonical draft and…

stages.title: Finalize Content And Record
stages.summary: Finalize the reviewed canonical packet and durable memory, then prepare only the publication jobs permitted by publication_intent.

stages.title: Execute Publication
stages.summary: Publish or stage only the jobs selected by publication_intent and target_channels, then record distribution results.

workflows.name: Communications Inbox Review
workflows.description: A bounded inbox review with selected messages summarized, classified, and turned into explicit follow-up only when authorized.
workflows.audience: Operations and support teams reviewing shared inboxes
workflows.problem: A busy mailbox mixes routine messages, sensitive content, and work requests that need different treatment.
workflows.outcome: A bounded inbox review with selected messages summarized, classified, and turned into explicit follow-up only when authorized.
operatorPath: Choose the mailbox, time window, and review rules.
Define which messages may create work, be marked, or receive a response.
Review the shortlist and approve any external follow-up.
agentPath: Fetch only the bounded message set and preserve mailbox privacy rules.
Summarize and classify with uncertainty visible.
Prepare or create approved follow-up work and leave unrelated messages untouched.
recovery: If mailbox access or UID state is stale, stop and refresh instead of guessing message identity.
If classification is uncertain, leave the message unchanged and flag it for review.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a read-only shortlist when message mutation or response was not authorized.
prerequisites: A connected mailbox profile
A bounded mailbox and time or UID scope
Rules for privacy, marking, replies, and work creation
proof: Mutation or follow-up receipts only where approved
whenToUse: A project needs an agent to inspect recent email messages and decide which ones need action.
The agent should reuse existing communication-message resources and prior request history before fetching more.

stages.title: Orient From Mailbox History
stages.summary: Read prior messages, requests, and the current goal.

stages.title: Search Bounded Messages
stages.summary: Search the configured mailbox by explicit criteria and limit.

stages.title: Fetch Selected Messages
stages.summary: Fetch only selected fields/body previews for candidate UIDs.

stages.title: Create Or Prepare Work
stages.summary: Create or prepare generic agent requests only for messages that need follow-up.

stages.title: Update Mailbox Status
stages.summary: Mark reviewed messages seen only when approval and policy allow it.

workflows.name: Communications Outbound Notification
workflows.description: One approved notification delivered to a named destination with an auditable source and receipt.
workflows.audience: Teams sending operational updates after completed work
workflows.problem: Status updates are easy to send to the wrong audience, at the wrong time, or without evidence that the underlying work is complete.
workflows.outcome: One approved notification delivered to a named destination with an auditable source and receipt.
workflows.whyAi: AI can turn a completed run or decision into audience-appropriate copy, while route and send policy control delivery.
operatorPath: Name the completed result, audience, destination, and urgency.
Review the message when policy requires it.
Approve the send or keep the message as a draft.
agentPath: Verify the source result and named delivery target.
Draft only what the evidence supports and resolve the allowed route.
Send once with idempotency and record the provider receipt.
recovery: If the target or route is unclear, keep the draft and ask for the missing destination.
If delivery status is uncertain, inspect the existing action receipt before retrying.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with an approved draft when sending was not authorized.
prerequisites: A completed source result or decision
A named communication target and allowed route
Audience and approval policy
proof: Provider message id and idempotency state
whenToUse: A run, decision, or agent action needs a human-facing notification.
The agent has explicit recipients/channels and approved message content.

stages.title: Confirm Notification Need
stages.summary: Read the goal and recent action calls before composing a notification.

stages.title: Compose Explicit Payload
stages.summary: Produce exact recipient/channel, subject, body, and optional origin refs.

stages.title: Send Notification
stages.summary: Execute the explicitly granted send action.

workflows.name: Customer Feedback Intake
workflows.description: One route-approved Slack thread containing the complete feedback packet and safe refs for investigation.
workflows.audience: Support and product teams receiving feedback across communication channels
workflows.problem: Customer feedback arriving across chat or email can lose media, privacy context, and ownership before anyone investigates it.
workflows.outcome: One route-approved Slack thread containing the complete feedback packet and safe refs for investigation.
workflows.whyAi: AI can normalize several communication formats and preserve context, while StackOS enforces the configured route and delivery boundary.
operatorPath: Choose the approved source-to-Slack route and the canonical support destination.
Set privacy, redaction, media retention, and task-creation policy.
Send feedback through an approved surface or provide safe message and media refs.
agentPath: Verify the source, route, destination, media inventory, and data-sharing boundary.
Normalize the report without diagnosing it or dropping allowed media.
Create one complete Slack handoff, add the intake signal, and preserve investigation refs.
recovery: Stop before sending if any required media cannot be forwarded safely.
On partial provider failure, avoid duplicate sends and resume from the stored delivery receipt.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a missing-route or privacy question before copying customer content.

handoffs.when: The complete feedback packet exists in the approved Slack thread.

prerequisites: A named source surface and message or media refs
An approved cross-surface route and Slack target
Privacy, redaction, retention, and media policy
whenToUse: Customer feedback or a support symptom arrives outside the canonical Slack support thread.
A project needs a single Slack thread before support investigation starts.
Source media, reporter context, route context, and safe message refs must be preserved for later agents.

stages.title: Capture Feedback
stages.summary: Read the inbound request and source refs, including media inventory and route hints.

stages.title: Establish Canonical Thread
stages.summary: Reuse a Slack-origin thread or create one route-approved Slack handoff that preserves source context and media.

stages.title: Add Intake Reaction
stages.summary: Mark the canonical Slack message with a reaction that indicates support intake has started.

stages.title: Prepare Investigation Handoff
stages.summary: Return the refs and concise support context needed to start support.issue-investigation without re-discovering the source message.

workflows.name: Delivery Task Handoff
workflows.description: A delivery-ready tracker task linked back to the approved support thread and handed to engineering without changing the product.
workflows.audience: Support and engineering teams converting verified issues into delivery work
workflows.problem: Support conclusions lose evidence or create premature engineering tasks when investigation, operator authorization, and delivery acceptance are mixed together.
workflows.outcome: A delivery-ready tracker task linked back to the approved support thread and handed to engineering without changing the product.
workflows.whyAi: AI can translate an evidence-backed conclusion into complete delivery context while the operator retains task-creation authority.
operatorPath: Review the completed support conclusion in the canonical Slack thread.
Give the required same-thread instruction to create delivery work.
Inspect the created task link and engineering handoff.
agentPath: Verify the investigation is complete and the same-thread authorization is current.
Create the task with reproduction, impact, evidence, acceptance criteria, and dependencies.
Reply with the task ref, add the task-created signal, and hand off to engineering.
recovery: If authorization or required delivery context is missing, ask in the same thread and create nothing.
If acknowledgement fails after task creation, reuse the existing task ref rather than duplicating work.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a same-thread clarification when task creation was not explicitly authorized.

handoffs.when: The delivery-ready task exists and implementation is authorized.

prerequisites: A completed support conclusion in the canonical Slack thread
A current same-thread instruction authorizing task creation
Project workflow and tracker context
whenToUse: A support.issue-investigation conclusion is posted and the operator replies in the same canonical thread asking for delivery work.
The task must preserve source, thread, media, conclusion, instruction, and handoff refs.
Engineering delivery should start from tracker state rather than chat-only context.

stages.title: Confirm Thread Instruction
stages.summary: Re-read the canonical thread and confirm the task instruction belongs to the same thread as the support conclusion.

stages.title: Create Delivery Task
stages.summary: Create delivery-ready tracker work with cause, scope, acceptance criteria, verification expectations, and preserved chat refs.

stages.title: Post Task Handoff
stages.summary: Reply in the canonical thread with the task link/key, scoped delivery summary, and engineering.tracked-delivery handoff.

stages.title: Add Task-Created Reaction
stages.summary: Add a semantically matching reaction to the canonical message only after task creation succeeds.

workflows.name: Engineering Tracked Delivery
workflows.description: A delivered change with an explicit flow, truthful tracker state, proportionate verification, independent review, and release evidence.
workflows.audience: Product and engineering teams delivering repository changes
workflows.problem: Engineering requests fail when requirements, dependencies, verification, and review live only in chat or are compressed into implementation.
workflows.outcome: A delivered change with an explicit flow, truthful tracker state, proportionate verification, independent review, and release evidence.
workflows.whyAi: AI can coordinate discovery, implementation, testing, and review across a long-running change while StackOS preserves dependencies and proof.
operatorPath: Describe the desired outcome, constraints, and authority boundary.
Review material design choices or production-risk gates.
Inspect verification, review findings, and residual risk before release.
agentPath: Define requirements and affected flows before planning tickets.
Discover the real code and system boundaries, then design the approach and proof plan.
Deliver through dependency-linked tickets, run the planned verification, and adjudicate independent review.
Audit tracker truth and close only when evidence matches the result.
recovery: Record blockers in the run and tracker instead of hiding them in chat.
Use run consistency and recovery operations to resume a blocked step without losing prior handoffs.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after a reviewed design or diagnosis when implementation authority was not granted.
prerequisites: Repository instructions and source access
A desired outcome and acceptance boundary
Authority for the requested file or system changes
whenToUse: A user asks for engineering work that needs planning, implementation, review, and signoff.
A user explicitly asks to use a workflow, engineering workflow, StackOS workflow, "the workflow", or to follow the SDLC for engineering delivery.
A user asks for release-grade delivery or equivalent lifecycle language that implies requirements-to-release enforcement.
Work should be visible as tracker tasks/tickets with dependencies, blockers, evidence, and release state.
A project wants host-local agents configured from SDLC presets for a concrete delivery flow.
The change touches contracts, tests, UI, data, provider behavior, MCP/REST/CLI operation surfaces, docs, or release risk.

stages.title: Scope Work
stages.summary: Restate the engineering goal, delivery mode, constraints, project setup, host-agent format, and missing context.

stages.title: Define Requirements And Flows
stages.summary: Convert user intent into explicit user, data, system, and business flows, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and evidence expectations.

stages.title: Discover Impact
stages.summary: Map affected scopes, real execution paths, ownership boundaries, downstream readers, tests, docs, and signoff fallout.

stages.title: Plan Tracker Tickets
stages.summary: Create or update tracker tasks/tickets with dependencies, sequencing, blockers, owned scopes, and definition of done.

stages.title: Design Approach
stages.summary: Identify canonical ownership, shared operations, data invariants, adapter surfaces, grants, docs, tests, rollout, and rollback implications.

stages.title: Review Design
stages.summary: Challenge the requirements, impact map, ticket plan, and design before implementation.

stages.title: Design Tests, TDD, And Manual Scenarios
stages.summary: Own the full proof plan before delivery: TDD/red-first automated tests, verification commands, agent-executed E2E/manual flow scenarios, expected…

stages.title: Deliver Tickets
stages.summary: Claim ready tracker tickets, implement one by one, update status, and record focused verification evidence.

stages.title: Verify Delivery
stages.summary: Run and report the mandatory verification stack for the actual diff and risk profile.

stages.title: Review Delivery
stages.summary: Review completed delivery across behavior, QA, integration contracts, security boundaries, docs, tracker evidence, and release risk.

stages.title: Audit Tracker Truth
stages.summary: Confirm tracker state, run-plan state, docs, tests, evidence, and the actual diff agree before closeout.

stages.title: Release Closeout
stages.summary: Confirm final signoff, docs, known limitations, commit/release state, rollback/watch notes, and post-release checks.

workflows.name: GTM Account Research
workflows.description: A source-linked account brief focused on fit, timing, risks, and the next useful action.
workflows.audience: Sales and go-to-market teams researching target accounts
workflows.problem: Account research often becomes a generic company summary that does not help someone make a real go-to-market decision.
workflows.outcome: A source-linked account brief focused on fit, timing, risks, and the next useful action.
workflows.whyAi: AI can combine stored account context with bounded current research while preserving source and confidence distinctions.
operatorPath: Name the account, offer, audience, and decision the brief should support.
Choose approved internal and web sources.
Review uncertain findings before they become outreach claims.
agentPath: Load existing account and project context before external research.
Research only the missing decision-relevant facts and retain source refs.
Separate observations, inferences, gaps, and recommended next actions.
recovery: If entity identity is ambiguous, stop before merging sources.
If live research is unavailable, return a useful stored-context brief with explicit gaps.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after the research brief; outreach requires a separate workflow and approval.

handoffs.when: The account is qualified and approved for outreach.

prerequisites: A named account and decision to support
Offer and fit criteria
Approved internal and external research sources
proof: Open questions and recommended next action
whenToUse: A project needs account or company research before enrichment, outbound, CRM updates, or pipeline review.
The agent should reuse prior account context and learnings before collecting more evidence.

stages.title: Orient From Account History
stages.summary: Read existing records, learnings, decisions, and the current research goal.

stages.title: Collect Evidence
stages.summary: Use granted retrieval actions only for evidence gaps the agent identifies.

stages.title: Store Research
stages.summary: Persist reviewed account, company, and contact context with provenance.

stages.title: Capture Learning
stages.summary: Record reusable GTM lessons only when evidence is explicit.

workflows.name: GTM CRM Hygiene Pass
workflows.description: A reviewed hygiene report with narrowly approved corrections and an audit of every changed record.
workflows.audience: Revenue operations teams maintaining CRM quality
workflows.problem: Incomplete, duplicated, or inconsistent CRM records make reporting and follow-up unreliable, but broad cleanup can damage good data.
workflows.outcome: A reviewed hygiene report with narrowly approved corrections and an audit of every changed record.
operatorPath: Choose the CRM scope and hygiene rules.
Set fields that may be changed automatically and fields that require review.
Approve the proposed correction set before any provider write.
agentPath: Inspect the bounded record set and identify issues without mutating it.
Group duplicates, missing fields, stale values, and policy conflicts with evidence.
Apply only approved corrections and verify the provider result.
recovery: On ambiguous identity or field authority, leave the record unchanged.
On partial provider failure, reconcile receipts before retrying remaining records.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a read-only hygiene report when mutation is not authorized.
prerequisites: A connected CRM or approved record export
A bounded record scope
Field ownership, duplicate, and mutation rules
whenToUse: CRM records need cleanup, deduping notes, field updates, or task creation.

stages.title: Orient From CRM Context
stages.summary: Read scope, policy, stored records, and prior hygiene lessons.

stages.title: Prepare Cleanup
stages.summary: Identify field updates, notes, tasks, duplicates, and records requiring human review.

stages.title: Optional Apply Cleanup
stages.summary: Execute approved CRM write actions only when granted by the concrete run plan.

workflows.name: GTM Lead Enrichment And Scoring
workflows.description: A source-linked enrichment and scoring result with confidence, disqualification reasons, and no silent field invention.
workflows.audience: Growth and sales teams enriching and prioritizing leads
workflows.problem: Lead scoring becomes untrustworthy when enrichment sources, missing values, and scoring assumptions are hidden.
workflows.outcome: A source-linked enrichment and scoring result with confidence, disqualification reasons, and no silent field invention.
operatorPath: Provide the lead set, ideal-customer criteria, and scoring policy.
Choose approved enrichment sources and how missing data should be treated.
Review threshold or policy changes before they affect routing.
agentPath: Validate lead identity and existing fields before enrichment.
Use approved sources, preserve provenance, and distinguish missing from negative evidence.
Apply the explicit scoring policy and store reasons, confidence, and follow-up.
recovery: Do not merge ambiguous people or companies.
If a provider is unavailable, score only where the policy permits missing data and flag the rest.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with enriched records and proposed scores when routing was not authorized.

handoffs.when: Leads meet the reviewed qualification threshold and outreach is approved.

prerequisites: A bounded lead set
Ideal-customer and scoring criteria
Approved sources and missing-data policy
whenToUse: A lead or account list needs enrichment, qualification, or scoring before CRM or outbound work.

stages.title: Orient From Lead Context
stages.summary: Read existing lead records, enrichment state, scoring policy, and prior lessons.

stages.title: Run Enrichment
stages.summary: Execute only the provider enrichment actions granted by the concrete run plan.

stages.title: Score And Store
stages.summary: Persist enrichment records, lead scores, qualification notes, and provenance.

workflows.name: GTM Outbound Sequence Preparation
workflows.description: A reviewed, project-voiced outreach sequence and send-readiness packet without sending it implicitly.
workflows.audience: Go-to-market teams preparing evidence-based outreach
workflows.problem: Outbound messages become generic or risky when qualification, personalization evidence, compliance, and send authorization are collapsed into one drafting step.
workflows.outcome: A reviewed, project-voiced outreach sequence and send-readiness packet without sending it implicitly.
workflows.whyAi: AI can adapt a sequence across qualified accounts while keeping every personalization claim and compliance decision traceable.
operatorPath: Choose the qualified audience, offer, channel, and desired response.
Set voice, claim, compliance, frequency, and exclusion rules.
Review representative messages and approve send readiness separately.
agentPath: Verify qualification and personalization evidence for each recipient.
Draft within the project voice and channel contract without inventing familiarity.
Run compliance and claim checks, then return a send-readiness packet.
recovery: Disqualify or hold recipients with insufficient evidence rather than filling gaps.
Keep the packet staged when a target, route, or send approval is missing.
safeStoppingPoints: This workflow stops at send readiness; delivery requires explicit authorization.
prerequisites: A qualified recipient set
Project voice and approved offer claims
Compliance, exclusion, cadence, and channel rules
whenToUse: A reviewed lead or account set should be prepared for Outreach, Salesloft, or user-owned sequence tooling.

stages.title: Orient From Outbound Context
stages.summary: Read lead context, prior sequences, touchpoints, send policy, and outbound lessons.

stages.title: Prepare Sequence
stages.summary: Prepare reviewed prospect groups, messaging refs, task needs, and approval notes.

stages.title: Optional Outbound Actions
stages.summary: Execute approved provider sequence, mail, or calendar actions only when granted by the concrete run plan.

workflows.name: GTM Pipeline Risk Review
workflows.description: An evidence-backed risk review with prioritized follow-up, owners, and uncertainty clearly separated from fact.
workflows.audience: Sales leaders and revenue operations teams reviewing pipeline risk
workflows.problem: Pipeline reviews overreact to stale CRM fields or produce a list of vague concerns without owners and evidence.
workflows.outcome: An evidence-backed risk review with prioritized follow-up, owners, and uncertainty clearly separated from fact.
operatorPath: Choose the pipeline segment, review window, and business thresholds.
Confirm the sources and definitions for stage, value, activity, and risk.
Review proposed follow-up before any CRM or communication action.
agentPath: Load the bounded pipeline snapshot and relevant activity context.
Test each risk signal against the project policy and source freshness.
Prioritize risks, name evidence and uncertainty, and prepare owner-specific follow-up.
recovery: If source freshness is insufficient, mark the conclusion provisional.
Do not mutate CRM or contact owners without the required approval and route.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with the reviewed risk report when follow-up execution was not authorized.

handoffs.when: A material account risk requires fresh external or internal research.

prerequisites: A bounded pipeline snapshot
Freshness and risk definitions
Ownership and follow-up policy
whenToUse: A pipeline, segment, opportunity set, or forecast needs a bounded risk review.

stages.title: Orient From Pipeline Context
stages.summary: Read review scope, targets, stored snapshots, learnings, and decisions.

stages.title: Fetch Metric Gaps
stages.summary: Use granted read actions only when stored pipeline evidence is insufficient.

stages.title: Diagnose Risks
stages.summary: Separate likely stage, activity, account, deal, ownership, and timing risks.

workflows.name: Issue Investigation
workflows.description: An evidence-backed conclusion in the canonical support thread: verified issue, bounded uncertainty, expected behavior, or not a product defect.
workflows.audience: Support and product teams investigating customer-reported issues
workflows.problem: Customer reports are either dismissed too early or turned into engineering work before expected behavior, reproduction, and impact are established.
workflows.outcome: An evidence-backed conclusion in the canonical support thread: verified issue, bounded uncertainty, expected behavior, or not a product defect.
workflows.whyAi: AI can connect the full conversation, media, product context, and reproduction evidence while keeping task creation outside the investigator's authority.
operatorPath: Provide the canonical thread and safe media refs.
Answer bounded clarification questions about expected behavior, environment, timing, and impact.
Review the conclusion and decide whether delivery work should be created.
agentPath: Read the full thread and media before forming a hypothesis.
Separate report, expectation, evidence, reproduction, scope, and uncertainty.
Ask only bounded clarifications, investigate safely, and post the conclusion in the same thread.
Do not create delivery tasks.
recovery: If evidence or access is missing, state exactly what is unverified and ask the smallest useful question.
If a provider reply fails, preserve the conclusion and retry from the stored thread and action receipt.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with bounded uncertainty; task creation requires a separate workflow and operator instruction.

handoffs.when: The conclusion supports delivery work and the operator authorizes task creation in the same thread.

prerequisites: A canonical Slack thread with the complete feedback packet
Expected versus actual behavior, environment, timing, and impact context
Safe access to relevant media or reproduction evidence
proof: No task creation by the investigator
whenToUse: A canonical Slack support thread exists and the issue needs diagnosis before delivery work is created.
The agent must read the whole support thread, include media/context, and produce a root-cause or bounded-uncertainty conclusion.
The operator needs a clear support conclusion before deciding whether to create delivery work.

stages.title: Read Canonical Thread
stages.summary: Read the full stored thread and source context before forming hypotheses.

stages.title: Clarify Missing Context
stages.summary: Ask a same-thread clarification only when missing evidence blocks a safe conclusion; otherwise record why clarification was skipped.

stages.title: Investigate Issue
stages.summary: Trace the symptom through project evidence, validate or bound root cause, and identify affected surfaces and delivery direction.

stages.title: Post Support Conclusion
stages.summary: Post the evidence-backed conclusion in the canonical thread and tell the operator that task creation is a separate instructed handoff.

workflows.name: Landing Page And Creative Experiment
workflows.description: A launch-ready experiment that connects named variants, destination changes, tracking, guardrails, and one approved provider route.
workflows.audience: Growth teams testing paid creative and landing pages together
workflows.problem: Creative and landing-page changes are often tested together without a clean hypothesis, dependency map, or observation plan.
workflows.outcome: A launch-ready experiment that connects named variants, destination changes, tracking, guardrails, and one approved provider route.
workflows.whyAi: AI can coordinate page, creative, tracking, and media dependencies while preserving explicit experiment and approval decisions.
operatorPath: State the hypothesis, variants, metric targets, traffic constraints, and observation window.
Confirm implementation and tracking readiness.
Approve campaign changes through the selected provider route.
agentPath: Validate that the proposed variants isolate a useful question.
Map page, creative, tracking, and campaign dependencies.
Prepare the experiment and QA plan; mutate the campaign only after approval.
Store the experiment record and follow-up observation criteria.
recovery: If variants or tracking cannot support the hypothesis, return to design instead of launching.
If provider launch fails, preserve staged assets and reconcile campaign state before retry.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a launch-ready experiment plan when campaign mutation is not approved.

handoffs.when: The experiment observation window has enough data for diagnosis.

prerequisites: A clear hypothesis and named variants
Metric targets, traffic policy, and observation window
Verified page and tracking readiness
One provider route and approval only for launch
proof: Launch approval and provider receipt when executed
whenToUse: The agent needs to design a paid media experiment involving landing page and creative combinations.
The project needs a durable experiment record before campaign changes are made.

stages.title: Orient From Experiment History
stages.summary: Read existing experiments, related resources, prior lessons, and current inputs.

stages.title: Define Experiment Setup
stages.summary: Define the variant set, metric targets, observation windows, and stopping rules.

stages.title: Record Experiment
stages.summary: Persist the experiment setup before any campaign or ad changes.

stages.title: Optional Launch Update
stages.summary: Execute approved campaign or ad updates only when granted by the concrete run plan.

workflows.name: Marketing Campaign Production
workflows.description: A coherent campaign package with approved messaging, generated assets, landing-page variants, a visual review, and a local evidence gallery.
workflows.audience: Marketing teams producing multi-asset campaigns
workflows.problem: Campaign production fragments the brief, messaging, media, landing pages, and review across disconnected tools and approvals.
workflows.outcome: A coherent campaign package with approved messaging, generated assets, landing-page variants, a visual review, and a local evidence gallery.
workflows.whyAi: AI can coordinate several creative and mechanical specialties while retaining one brief, shared constraints, and independent visual signoff.
operatorPath: Provide the product, audience, offer, brand constraints, and desired media.
Confirm the campaign plan and generation budget before production.
Review the final gallery and approve or reject each deliverable.
agentPath: Resolve the brief and brand evidence before choosing concepts or tools.
Plan media and page variants, then produce through explicit provider routes.
Verify requirements, run independent visual signoff, and assemble the evidence gallery.
recovery: If brand or product evidence is missing, pause before creative production.
Preserve completed assets and provider receipts when one route fails; resume only the blocked branch.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after the confirmed production plan when generation spend was not approved.

handoffs.when: The approved campaign package is intended for a paid-media launch.

prerequisites: A product brief, audience, offer, and brand profile
Approved product media and disclosure boundaries
Generation budget and provider route for selected media
whenToUse: A user asks to produce a marketing campaign from a brand identity, product description, and product photo.
Campaign media (images, short videos), landing page variants, and a reviewable gallery should be produced together with durable state and signoff evidence.
Landing page A/B variants and their media requirements should be planned and approved before any generation spend.

stages.title: Intake Campaign Brief
stages.summary: Convert operator inputs into a complete structured campaign brief with persisted input artifacts and an explicit media request.

stages.title: Set Up Campaign Workspace
stages.summary: Create the repository campaign workspace and scaffold campaign.md as the operator-readable campaign ledger.

stages.title: Plan Campaign And Media Manifest
stages.summary: Produce the campaign plan that production executes and signoff verifies; media manifest, landing page variant plans, gallery plan, and cost estimate.

stages.title: Produce Campaign Media
stages.summary: Execute approved manifest entries through granted generation actions and materialize results into the campaign workspace.

stages.title: Build Landing Page Variants
stages.summary: Build each planned variant as a self-contained static page in the workspace using produced media and the approved angle.

stages.title: Visual Signoff
stages.summary: Verify every produced asset and page against the approved plan with rendered evidence and explicit per-item verdicts.

stages.title: Build Campaign Gallery
stages.summary: Build the local human-readable gallery page that presents everything the campaign produced to a non-engineer.

stages.title: Campaign Closeout
stages.summary: Reconcile state, finalize the campaign ledger, and hand the operator one front door to everything produced.

workflows.name: Media Buying Budget Reallocation Review
workflows.description: An evidence-backed budget recommendation, explicit approval boundary, and observation plan; mutations occur only when separately approved.
workflows.audience: Growth teams reviewing paid-media budget allocation
workflows.problem: Budget moves are tempting when performance shifts, but weak comparisons and missing guardrails can turn noise into spend changes.
workflows.outcome: An evidence-backed budget recommendation, explicit approval boundary, and observation plan; mutations occur only when separately approved.
workflows.whyAi: AI can compare several campaigns and hypotheses consistently while preserving the operator's budget judgment and rollback authority.
operatorPath: Choose campaigns, comparison window, KPI targets, and delivery constraints.
Set movement limits, approval owner, observation window, and rollback rule.
Review the recommendation before approving any account change.
agentPath: Validate measurement comparability before diagnosing allocation.
Model the proposed movement against performance, constraints, and uncertainty.
Return a recommendation first; execute only the selected provider route after approval.
Record the change and post-change observation plan.
recovery: When evidence is weak, recommend no change and name the missing observation.
On partial provider failure, reconcile actual account state before retry or rollback.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after the recommendation when budget mutation was not explicitly approved.

handoffs.when: The post-change observation window closes or performance diverges from the expected range.

prerequisites: Comparable campaign performance evidence
KPI targets and attribution window
Budget, delivery, approval, observation, and rollback guardrails
One selected provider route only when mutation is approved
proof: Provider receipt and observation plan when changed
whenToUse: A project needs to evaluate budget changes across campaigns, channels, audiences, or creative sets.
The agent should separate recommendation, approval, and optional external mutation.

stages.title: Orient From Budget History
stages.summary: Read budget policy, recent performance, prior budget changes, learnings, and decisions.

stages.title: Fetch Metric Gaps
stages.summary: Use granted read actions only when stored evidence is insufficient.

stages.title: Propose Changes
stages.summary: Produce a bounded reallocation recommendation with rationale, risk, and observation plan.

stages.title: Optional Apply Changes
stages.summary: Execute approved budget update actions only when granted by the concrete run plan.

workflows.name: Media Buying Campaign Launch
workflows.description: A provider-specific campaign plan that passes launch checks and creates external campaign objects only after approval.
workflows.audience: Paid-media teams launching campaigns through connected ad platforms
workflows.problem: Paid campaigns fail at launch when creative, destination, tracking, budget, and provider setup are checked separately or too late.
workflows.outcome: A provider-specific campaign plan that passes launch checks and creates external campaign objects only after approval.
workflows.whyAi: AI can keep the brief, creative, page, tracking, and provider contracts aligned while explicit actions retain spend and write controls.
operatorPath: Provide the campaign goal, audience, offer, assets, destination, and budget guardrails.
Select one provider route and resolve its connection.
Review the launch plan, tracking, and risk before approving external creation.
agentPath: Validate the brief and dependencies before selecting the concrete provider route.
Build the provider-specific structure and validate every action payload.
Hold the launch and budget approvals, then execute only the selected route.
Record safe provider refs and the first measurement window.
recovery: If no provider route is ready, keep the validated launch plan and return one setup path.
On partial creation, reconcile created objects before repair or rollback.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a validated launch plan when external creation or spend is not approved.

handoffs.when: The first meaningful measurement window has completed.

prerequisites: A campaign goal, audience, offer, assets, destination, and budget policy
Tracking and naming rules
One connected provider route
Launch and spend approval owners
proof: Provider object receipts and first measurement plan
whenToUse: A project needs to launch a paid media campaign across one or more configured channels.
The agent needs history, learnings, approvals, resources, and provider contracts before creating campaign structures.

stages.title: Orient From History
stages.summary: Read recent work, resources, learnings, experiments, and the current goal.

stages.title: Prepare Launch Shape
stages.summary: Define the channel path, campaign structure, creative needs, measurement window, and open risks.

stages.title: Create External Structures
stages.summary: Execute only approved provider actions granted by the concrete run plan.

stages.title: Record Launch State
stages.summary: Persist campaign, creative, landing page, and initial measurement records.

stages.title: Capture Learning
stages.summary: Record reusable setup lessons with evidence and provenance.

workflows.name: Media Buying Creative Variant Generation
workflows.description: A focused candidate set tied to an experiment hypothesis, with generation and provider upload kept behind separate approvals.
workflows.audience: Growth and creative teams developing paid-media variants
workflows.problem: Ad variants become expensive noise when they are generated without a hypothesis, offer boundary, destination fit, or upload decision.
workflows.outcome: A focused candidate set tied to an experiment hypothesis, with generation and provider upload kept behind separate approvals.
workflows.whyAi: AI can explore several evidence-based variants quickly while reviewers preserve brand, claim, and experimental discipline.
operatorPath: Provide the offer, audience hypotheses, destination, brand constraints, and channel preference.
Approve generation cost when new assets are needed.
Review the candidate set and approve any provider upload separately.
agentPath: Read prior creative and performance evidence before setting direction.
Define the hypothesis and bounded variant dimensions, then generate only the planned assets.
Review claims and destination fit; upload through one selected route only after approval.
recovery: Use operator-supplied assets when generation is unavailable or unnecessary.
Preserve reviewed candidates and stop when no upload route is ready.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a reviewed candidate set when upload was not requested or approved.

handoffs.when: The candidates require a measured creative-and-page experiment.
The candidates belong to a new approved campaign launch.

prerequisites: An offer, audience hypothesis, destination, and brand boundary
A bounded experiment or variation plan
Generation approval only when new assets are needed
One provider route only when upload is approved
proof: Upload receipts only for the selected route
whenToUse: A campaign needs new candidate creative concepts, copy angles, or generated assets.
The agent should combine historical learnings with explicit creative constraints.

stages.title: Orient From Creative History
stages.summary: Read recent creatives, learnings, active experiments, and constraints.

stages.title: Define Candidate Set
stages.summary: Create explicit candidate angles, copy needs, asset needs, and review criteria.

stages.title: Generate Or Attach Assets
stages.summary: Use granted utility actions or existing asset refs to create the candidate asset set.

stages.title: Store Reviewed Creatives
stages.summary: Persist reviewed candidate creative records and their provenance.

stages.title: Optional External Upload
stages.summary: Execute approved provider upload or campaign update actions only when granted by the concrete run plan.

workflows.name: Media Buying Performance Diagnosis
workflows.description: A source-linked diagnosis with ranked hypotheses, evidence gaps, and a recommendation for no change, budget review, creative work, or an experiment.
workflows.audience: Paid-media teams diagnosing performance changes before acting
workflows.problem: When paid performance worsens, teams jump to budget or creative changes before separating measurement, audience, channel, page, and market causes.
workflows.outcome: A source-linked diagnosis with ranked hypotheses, evidence gaps, and a recommendation for no change, budget review, creative work, or an experiment.
workflows.whyAi: AI can compare multiple causal categories and time windows systematically while keeping uncertainty and business judgment visible.
operatorPath: Name the affected accounts and campaigns, what changed, the KPI, and the comparison window.
Provide exports or select one read-only provider route.
Review the diagnosis before starting any mutation workflow.
agentPath: Validate measurement and comparison quality before explaining performance.
Use stored evidence or one ready metric-source route; unavailable alternatives stay optional.
Rank causal hypotheses, name counter-evidence and gaps, and recommend the smallest useful next step.
Stop after diagnosis unless the operator starts a follow-up workflow.
recovery: If evidence is insufficient, recommend no change and list the minimum missing data.
If a live provider is unavailable, use supplied exports or stored snapshots rather than blocking on every provider.
safeStoppingPoints: This workflow is recommendation-first and stops before budget, creative, page, or campaign mutation.

handoffs.when: Allocation is a supported cause and budget movement should be reviewed.
Creative fatigue or mismatch is a supported cause.
The evidence supports a measured creative or destination experiment.

prerequisites: Affected accounts and campaigns
The exact change date, KPI target, comparison window, and attribution assumptions
Stored snapshots, exports, or one ready read-only provider route
whenToUse: A campaign, channel, creative set, or audience needs a performance review.
The agent should retrieve only relevant historical fields before diagnosing.

stages.title: Orient From Performance Context
stages.summary: Read the review scope, metric targets, recent snapshots, related learnings, and active experiments.

stages.title: Fetch Additional Metrics
stages.summary: Execute only the selected read action that the concrete run plan grants for missing evidence.

stages.title: Diagnose Drivers
stages.summary: Compare evidence against targets and separate likely channel, audience, creative, landing page, and budget drivers.

stages.title: Record Followups
stages.summary: Store recommended follow-up resources, budget review notes, and learning candidates for later review without executing a mutation.

workflows.name: Project Memory Review
workflows.description: A bounded memory review that explains what the project knows, what remains uncertain, and the best next action.
workflows.audience: Any team resuming or orienting complex project work
workflows.problem: Projects accumulate runs, decisions, evidence, and unfinished work faster than an agent can safely reconstruct from chat.
workflows.outcome: A bounded memory review that explains what the project knows, what remains uncertain, and the best next action.
workflows.whyAi: AI can connect evidence across several project records while keeping source refs and uncertainty visible.
operatorPath: State the question or decision the memory review should support.
Confirm the project scope and desired persistence level.
Review the recommendation before starting any follow-up workflow.
agentPath: Check active tracker and run-plan state first so the review does not ignore or duplicate current work.
Query only the relevant project sources and preserve their refs.
Separate facts, decisions, stale assumptions, contradictions, and gaps.
Return a recommendation or a concrete run-plan proposal without executing it implicitly.
recovery: If the project scope or source authority is unclear, stop with a bounded clarification.
If records conflict, present both and name the decision needed.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after the recommendation unless the operator explicitly starts follow-up work.
prerequisites: A clear question or decision
An existing StackOS project with relevant stored context
whenToUse: The agent needs to understand recent project history before planning work.
A workflow should start from prior runs, learnings, experiments, and decisions.

stages.title: Clarify Goal And Scope
stages.summary: Restate the goal, assumptions, constraints, and missing inputs.

stages.title: Retrieve Bounded Context
stages.summary: Query only the declared fields and limits before reasoning.

stages.title: Propose Next Plan
stages.summary: Draft an agent-authored plan, explicit open questions, and any approval needs.

workflows.name: Rich Telegram Reply
workflows.description: One Telegram reply whose text, media, buttons, and thread context match the approved response contract.
workflows.audience: Teams replying to Telegram requests with media or buttons
workflows.problem: Rich replies can silently lose images, buttons, threading, or privacy boundaries when a platform feature is unavailable.
workflows.outcome: One Telegram reply whose text, media, buttons, and thread context match the approved response contract.
operatorPath: Provide the reply intent and approved media or action choices.
Confirm any button behavior that creates work or changes state.
Review the sent message and stored interaction refs.
agentPath: Load the originating request and Telegram surface policy.
Validate text, every media item, and button payload before any send.
Send without silent degradation and store message and interaction receipts.
recovery: Reject unsupported rich features instead of silently dropping them.
Use provider receipts and idempotency state to avoid duplicate replies after failure.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a validated reply packet when the origin or feature contract is incomplete.
prerequisites: A connected Telegram profile
An originating request and approved reply destination
Safe media refs and defined button behavior
whenToUse: A Telegram message created an agent_request and the response may need buttons or images.
The agent must respect the originating communication profile, chat, thread, and response policy.

stages.title: Read Origin Context
stages.summary: Inspect the request metadata, communication profile guidance, and thread context.

stages.title: Compose Response Payload
stages.summary: Decide exact text, image refs, and optional button payloads without embedding secrets.

stages.title: Send Telegram Response
stages.summary: Execute the explicit Telegram send action granted by the run plan.

workflows.name: SEO Content Refresh
workflows.description: A focused page refresh with traceable changes, preserved useful content, and a verification plan.
workflows.audience: SEO and content teams improving existing pages
workflows.problem: Content refreshes turn into broad rewrites when search intent, current performance, technical issues, and conversion purpose are not diagnosed first.
workflows.outcome: A focused page refresh with traceable changes, preserved useful content, and a verification plan.
workflows.whyAi: AI can compare search evidence, page structure, project context, and editorial constraints while keeping the final judgment and publication boundary explicit.
operatorPath: Choose the page, business goal, search intent, and performance window.
Provide approved analytics, query, and content sources.
Review the proposed changes before publication.
agentPath: Diagnose intent, performance, technical, content, and conversion gaps before editing.
Map each proposed change to evidence and preserve what already works.
Prepare or apply the bounded refresh, then verify content and technical acceptance criteria.
recovery: If query or performance evidence is missing, return a bounded diagnostic and data request.
If publication is not authorized, stop with a review-ready patch or content packet.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop with a review-ready refresh plan or patch when publication was not authorized.
prerequisites: A page, business goal, and target search intent
Approved performance and query evidence
Editorial, technical, and publication boundaries
whenToUse: Existing content needs a refresh diagnosis, evidence gathering, and recorded follow-through.

stages.title: Read History
stages.summary: Complete the read history stage.

stages.title: Diagnose Refresh Need
stages.summary: Complete the diagnose stage.

stages.title: Execute Change
stages.summary: Agent performs the chosen external or repository work using tools available in the current environment.

stages.title: Record Outcome
stages.summary: Complete the record outcome stage.

workflows.name: SEO Keyword Research
workflows.description: A prioritized opportunity map that separates near-term article ideas, supporting clusters, evidence, and topics to avoid.
workflows.audience: SEO and content teams choosing what to write next
workflows.problem: Keyword research produces broad high-volume lists when it is not anchored to the business, audience, product proximity, and existing authority.
workflows.outcome: A prioritized opportunity map that separates near-term article ideas, supporting clusters, evidence, and topics to avoid.
workflows.whyAi: AI can connect keyword data, SERP patterns, competitor evidence, and project context while the operator owns positioning and priority.
operatorPath: State the business goal, audience, product proximity, markets, and topics to avoid.
Choose approved keyword and competitor data sources.
Review the opportunity map and select content priorities.
agentPath: Translate the business brief into explicit opportunity criteria before collecting data.
Use approved keyword, SERP, and competitor sources and preserve provenance.
Cluster by intent and business fit, score with transparent tradeoffs, and propose concrete article angles.
recovery: If a provider is unavailable, use approved exports or stop with the exact missing dataset.
Do not fill the report with broad terms that fail the product-proximity or authority test.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after the prioritized opportunity map; article production is a separate approved job.

handoffs.when: A selected opportunity should become an evidence-backed branded article.

prerequisites: A business goal, audience, market, and product-proximity boundary
Topics to include and avoid
Connected keyword and SERP data sources or approved exports
proof: Concrete article angles and avoidance list
whenToUse: A project needs SEO opportunities from seed terms, competitors, or an agent-defined research angle.

stages.title: Orient From History
stages.summary: Read recent research, learnings, and the current project goal; if the user did not provide seed terms or competitors, ask a small number of…

stages.title: Collect Evidence
stages.summary: Execute only the provider calls the run plan grants and the agent still needs; preserve returned response-file paths, use schema.get with schema_ref only…

stages.title: Store Opportunities
stages.summary: Persist a useful list of keyword opportunities, not just one or two terms, with clusters, metrics, source refs, and a polished brief/CSV when the run…

stages.title: Capture Learning
stages.summary: Record reusable lessons or rejected patterns for future runs.

workflows.name: Telegram Callback Follow-Up
workflows.description: A context-aware follow-up that acknowledges the interaction and performs only the approved next action.
workflows.audience: Teams using interactive Telegram messages
workflows.problem: A button click can lose its original context or trigger an answer that overstates what happened.
workflows.outcome: A context-aware follow-up that acknowledges the interaction and performs only the approved next action.
workflows.whyAi: AI can interpret the stored interaction alongside the original request while provider tools remain limited to explicit acknowledgements and sends.
operatorPath: Define the button choices and what each choice is allowed to do.
Review any choice that can create work or send a material response.
Inspect the stored outcome when follow-up is complete.
agentPath: Load the original message, interaction record, and allowed callback policy.
Resolve the selected choice without treating callback data as an authoritative instruction by itself.
Acknowledge promptly, complete only the authorized work, and record the result.
recovery: If interaction context is missing or expired, acknowledge safely and request a fresh choice.
If downstream work is blocked, record the blocker without claiming completion.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after a safe acknowledgement when the selected action lacks context or approval.
prerequisites: A connected Telegram profile
Stored interaction context for each button
A clear policy for what each choice authorizes
whenToUse: A Telegram callback_query created an agent_request.
The agent needs to verify stored button state before acting on callback_data.

stages.title: Read Callback Context
stages.summary: Load the inbound callback, outbound button, and originating request metadata.

stages.title: Acknowledge Callback
stages.summary: Optionally clear Telegram client loading state with static acknowledgement text.

stages.title: Send Follow-Up When Needed
stages.summary: Send an origin-bound reply only if the agent decision requires it.

workflows.name: Website SEO Analysis
workflows.description: A source-ledgered site inventory, finding register, and prioritized roadmap that explains coverage and limitations without changing the website.
workflows.audience: Website owners, SEO teams, and agencies diagnosing search visibility and measurement quality
workflows.problem: SEO audits become generic checklists or unsupported scores when the analyst does not map the actual site, reconcile public pages with first-party measurement, and distinguish measured facts from observations and inference.
workflows.outcome: A source-ledgered site inventory, finding register, and prioritized roadmap that explains coverage and limitations without changing the website.
workflows.whyAi: AI can traverse representative templates, reconcile public, first-party, and third-party evidence, and synthesize a practical agency-style roadmap while keeping claims traceable.
operatorPath: Provide the website, business goal, important markets or sections, and any scope exclusions.
Connect Search Console, Analytics, Tag Manager, or research providers when available; the public-site analysis remains usable without them.
Review the evidence classification, limitations, findings, and prioritized roadmap before assigning fixes.
agentPath: Establish the canonical host, audit boundary, business goal, markets, date window, and sampling plan.
Map public pages, navigation, robots and sitemap signals, representative templates, links, metadata, structured data, and rendered behavior.
Select the smallest useful connected evidence set, preserving durable action receipts and recording unavailable, skipped, or failed sources as explicit gaps.
Reconcile evidence, challenge unsupported claims, and prioritize actions by impact, confidence, effort, dependencies, and validation path.
Store a compact analysis index plus durable report, inventory, and finding-register artifacts.
progressSignals: Public site map and representative templates inspected
recovery: If optional providers are unavailable, continue from public pages and record the missing evidence in the source ledger.
If part of the site is inaccessible, narrow the observed scope and label the limitation instead of claiming a complete crawl.
If the target itself cannot be reached or validated, stop with the exact access failure and evidence collected so far.
safeStoppingPoints: Stop after the analysis package; fixes, publishing, tag changes, and indexing requests require separately authorized work.

handoffs.when: A reviewed technical SEO finding should become repository delivery work.
A reviewed page-level content finding should become a bounded refresh.

prerequisites: A reachable website URL and the business purpose of the analysis
Optional target markets, priority sections, exclusions, and performance date window
Optional connected Search Console, GA4, GTM, Firecrawl, Ahrefs, DataForSEO, or Serper sources
proof: Source ledger with used, unavailable, skipped, and failed sources
Public site inventory and representative-template coverage
Typed evidence index and independently reviewed SEO finding register
Durable final report plus completeness-appropriate inventory, finding-register, and evidence artifacts
whenToUse: A user asks to audit, map, analyze, or diagnose a website's SEO.
A website needs an agency-style technical, on-page, content, measurement, and optional authority/competitive review.
Connected Search Console, Analytics, Tag Manager, or research tools should enrich a public-site analysis.

stages.title: Scope The Audit
stages.summary: Establish the canonical host, business goal, markets, priority/excluded sections, date window, robots/access constraints, expected site size, and a…

stages.title: Map The Public Site
stages.summary: Read the homepage, robots and sitemap signals, primary navigation, representative section/template pages, internal links, redirects/canonicals,…

stages.title: Collect Connected Evidence
stages.summary: Select the smallest useful set of ready Search Console, GA4, GTM, Ahrefs, DataForSEO, and Serper actions with explicit bounded inputs; inspect response…

stages.title: Reconcile Evidence And Diagnose
stages.summary: Reconcile public, first-party, third-party, and operator evidence into the site inventory and finding register across crawl/indexability,…

stages.title: Review And Prioritize
stages.summary: Independently challenge scope coverage and every unsupported or overstated claim, then prioritize accepted findings by impact, confidence, effort,…

stages.title: Finalize And Store The Analysis
stages.summary: Store the reviewed analysis package and one compact website-seo-analysis record whose evidence index, review summary, executive summary, counts,…

agents.name: Architecture Agent
agents.description: Designs implementation structure, ownership boundaries, schemas, operation contracts, and one-brain reuse before delivery.

agents.name: Brand Profile Architect
agents.description: Builds and maintains evidence-led brand profiles and durable voice guides from operator judgment, representative samples, and independent review.

agents.name: Branding Channel Strategist
agents.description: Owns angle, structure, and channel publication job decisions without executing publication.

agents.name: Branding Claim Auditor
agents.description: Read-only adversarial reviewer that enforces evidence-over-assertion.

agents.name: Branding Evidence Curator
agents.description: Conditionally interviews, researches, and captures operational receipts when dedicated evidence lineage is warranted.

agents.name: Branding Narrative Writer
agents.description: Drafts canonical pieces and channel renditions from cleared evidence in the project-adapted voice.

agents.name: Branding Sanitization Reviewer
agents.description: Read-only disclosure reviewer that fails closed with cleared or blocked verdicts.

agents.name: Branding Voice Reviewer
agents.description: Read-only reviewer for base voice, channel register delta, form contract, and kill tests.

agents.name: Campaign Brief Analyst
agents.description: Converts raw operator inputs into a complete, professional campaign brief resource with persisted input artifacts and a proposed campaign workspace slug.

agents.name: Campaign Creative Director
agents.description: Turns the approved brief into the campaign plan whose media manifest, page plans, and acceptance criteria form the traceability contract for production and signoff.

agents.name: Campaign Landing Page Builder
agents.description: Builds each planned landing page variant as a self-contained static HTML file in the campaign workspace and assembles gallery.html as the operator-facing index.

agents.name: Campaign Media Producer
agents.description: Executes approved image manifest entries through granted generation actions with drift control, set consistency, cost discipline, and truthful per-asset records; video requests are…

agents.name: Campaign Visual Signoff Reviewer
agents.description: Verifies every produced asset and page against the approved campaign plan's acceptance criteria with rendered evidence and per-item verdicts instead of rubber stamps.

agents.name: Codebase Explorer
agents.description: Read-only mapper for affected scopes, execution paths, ownership boundaries, and downstream fallout before code changes.

agents.name: Communications Callback Follow-Up Agent
agents.description: Handles callback or follow-up requests with origin context, route policy, and reply/send safety.

agents.name: Communications Customer Feedback Intake Agent
agents.description: Normalizes customer feedback from Slack, Telegram, or another channel into one route-approved canonical Slack support thread while preserving media and handoff refs.

agents.name: Communications Inbox Review Agent
agents.description: Reviews stored inbound communications, triages requests, and prepares safe workflow or reply handoffs.

agents.name: Communications Outbound Notification Agent
agents.description: Prepares and sends explicit provider-neutral outbound notifications through named targets.

agents.name: Communications Rich Telegram Reply Agent
agents.description: Sends or validates Telegram replies with rich features while preserving exact delivery semantics.

agents.name: Delivery Agent
agents.description: Implements tickets one by one, debugs root causes when checks fail, updates tracker state, verifies behavior, and preserves project conventions.

agents.name: GTM Account Research Agent
agents.description: Produces account research briefs with source clarity, ICP fit, risks, and outreach-ready handoff.

agents.name: GTM CRM Hygiene Agent
agents.description: Plans and reviews CRM cleanup with field-level atomic updates, duplicates, and audit visibility.

agents.name: GTM Lead Enrichment Scoring Agent
agents.description: Enriches and scores leads according to project-specific fields, confidence, and review policy.

agents.name: GTM Outbound Sequence Agent
agents.description: Prepares outbound sequence content and targeting with research, compliance, and approval context.

agents.name: GTM Pipeline Risk Review Agent
agents.description: Reviews pipeline risk, stale opportunities, and next actions using CRM context and project-specific stage rules.

agents.name: Landing Page Creative Experiment Agent
agents.description: Coordinates landing-page and creative experiment planning across UX, media, tracking, and review.

agents.name: Media Budget Reallocation Agent
agents.description: Reviews budget movement proposals with evidence, constraints, and approval awareness.

agents.name: Media Campaign Launch Agent
agents.description: Prepares paid media campaign launches with explicit assumptions, approvals, and provider-action boundaries.

agents.name: Media Creative Variant Agent
agents.description: Generates and reviews creative variant plans tied to hypothesis, brand constraints, and measurement.

agents.name: Media Performance Diagnosis Agent
agents.description: Diagnoses media performance using bounded evidence, live-data caveats, and prioritized next checks.

agents.name: Planning Agent
agents.description: Turns scoped work into tracker tasks, dependencies, ticket slices, acceptance criteria, and verification strategy.

agents.name: Project Memory Review Agent
agents.description: Reviews bounded StackOS project memory and prepares a grounded next-step recommendation.

agents.name: Release/Ops Agent
agents.description: Handles release readiness, rollout notes, post-release checks, and operational cleanup.

agents.name: Requirements Flow Definer
agents.description: Turns broad intent into explicit project-adapted user, data, system, and business flows, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and evidence expectations before design begins.

agents.name: Review Agent
agents.description: Reviews designs and completed delivery across behavior, flow-regression coverage, QA, integration contracts, security, tracker evidence, and release risk.

agents.name: SEO Content Refresh Agent
agents.description: Reviews stale or underperforming content and prepares a refresh plan with source-backed changes.

agents.name: SEO Keyword Research Agent
agents.description: Plans and reviews keyword discovery workflows with evidence, constraints, and content opportunity handoff.

agents.name: Support Delivery Handoff Agent
agents.description: Converts a support conclusion and same-thread operator instruction into delivery-ready tracker work with durable chat refs.

agents.name: Support Issue Investigator
agents.description: Investigates the full canonical support thread and project evidence, asks bounded clarification questions, and posts a thread-bound conclusion without implementing fixes.

agents.name: Test Designer
agents.description: Maps flow design, requirements, and design risks to focused tests, proof surfaces, TDD gates, agent-executed E2E/manual proof depth, and signoff evidence.

agents.name: Trackbooth Agent API Operator
agents.description: Operates Trackbooth Agent API inventory only through StackOS manual catalog sync, generated actions, and daemon-held API-key auth.

agents.name: Website SEO Analysis Agent
agents.description: Maps a website, reconciles public and connected evidence, and produces one independently reviewed finding register and prioritized roadmap without inventing measurements.

agents.name: Workflow Author
agents.description: Designs reusable StackOS workflow contracts, project workflow extensions, and concrete run plans from project-safe context.

orchestrators.name: Brand Orchestrator
orchestrators.description: Main-agent operating loop for evidence-led brand-foundation setup and voice-consistent content production, review, and optional publication.

orchestrators.name: Campaign Production Orchestrator
orchestrators.description: Generic main-agent orchestration preset for end-to-end marketing campaign production from intake brief through plan approval, media production, landing pages, visual signoff, and gallery…

orchestrators.name: Delivery Orchestrator
orchestrators.description: Generic main-agent SDLC delivery orchestration preset for project-adapted flow design, planning, tracker/run-plan coordination, reviewer selection, E2E/manual verification matrices,…

orchestrators.name: Workflow Orchestrator
orchestrators.description: Main-agent operating loop for workflows that do not need a domain-specific orchestrator.
